background_template: {name} has been feeding {location} since {founded}. Forty covers, a short menu that follows the market, and a pass that runs hot from seven. {quirk}
neighbourhoods: the old bank building on the square
a converted chapel off the high street
the ground floor of the Corn Exchange
a candlelit room above the wine shop
the end of the harbour row
a dining room behind the butcher's
quirks: The menu is written by hand every morning and photographed badly every lunchtime.
Table nine wobbles. It has always wobbled. Regulars request it.
The chef refuses to serve anything with truffle oil and will explain why at length.
Sunday lunch sells out by Thursday and causes genuine family arguments.
The wine list is thirty bottles long and the owner has opinions on all thirty.
There's a dessert that isn't on the menu. Everyone knows about it anyway.
pains: the phone rings all through service with questions the menu could answer
people arrive expecting the tasting menu on a Tuesday, which is not a thing
we rewrite the menu weekly and the version people find online is always last month's
half the bookings want to know the vegetarian options before they'll commit
nobody can find our opening hours, so they ring — during service

products.name: Sourdough & Cultured Butter
products.description: Baked at four. Gone by nine.

products.name: Padrón Peppers
products.description: One in ten is a surprise.

products.name: Smoked Mackerel Pâté
products.description: With pickles and toast. Sharp on purpose.

products.name: Braised Beef Shin, Mash & Greens
products.description: Six hours in the oven. Falls apart on cue.

products.name: Wild Mushroom & Ale Pie
products.description: The vegetarian one that converts carnivores.

products.name: Hispi Cabbage, Anchovy Butter
products.description: A side that thinks it's a main.

products.name: Sticky Toffee Pudding
products.description: Non-negotiable. Never coming off the menu.

products.name: Chocolate & Sea Salt Tart
products.description: Thin, dark, and gone in four bites.

awkward.name: Slow-Roast Salt Marsh Lamb Shoulder for Two, Anchovy & Rosemary — Sharing Only, Pre-Order at Weekends
awkward.description: The centrepiece. Order it when you book.

awkward.name: Crème Brûlée à l'Ancienne
awkward.description: Cracked tableside. Weekends only, until it runs out.

features.name: menu listing
features.description: The menu, on the page, as text — every dish, every price, organised by course. This is the whole job.

features.name: booking enquiry form
features.description: A way to ask about tables, dates, and the lamb shoulder without ringing during service.

features.name: opening hours
features.description: When we're open, where people will see it. We close Mondays and the phone doesn't know that.

features.name: course filter
features.description: Let people jump to starters, mains, or desserts without scrolling the lot.

features.name: allergen information
features.description: Allergens per dish, clearly. We take this seriously and the page has to as well.

features.name: navigation
features.description: A way to move around the page. Nothing fancy.

constraints: It has to work on a phone. People check menus in the car park.
Our brand colour is #7B2D26. Please use it.
No PDFs. The menu goes on the page as text, not a download.
Don't invent dishes. The kitchen has enough opinions already.
The prices on the page have to match the file we sent. To the penny.
No background music. We beg you.
